items.verseEn: And the producers of sparks [when] striking
Indeed mankind, to his Lord, is ungrateful.

items.verse: مُّذَبْذَبِينَ بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ لَآ إِلَىٰ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ وَلَآ إِلَىٰ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ ۚ وَمَن يُضْلِلِ ٱللَّهُ فَلَن تَجِدَ لَهُۥ سَبِيلًۭا
items.verseEn: Wavering between them, [belonging] neither to these [i.e., the believers] nor to those [i.e., the disbelievers]. And whoever Allāh sends astray - never will you find for him a way.

items.verse: إِنَّا جَعَلْنَا فِىٓ أَعْنَـٰقِهِمْ أَغْلَـٰلًۭا فَهِىَ إِلَى ٱلْأَذْقَانِ فَهُم مُّقْمَحُونَ
items.verseEn: Indeed, We have put shackles on their necks, and they are to their chins, so they are with heads [kept] aloft.
And he drew lots and was among the losers.

items.verse: وَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَكَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا وَلِقَآئِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ فِى ٱلْعَذَابِ مُحْضَرُونَ
items.verseEn: But as for those who disbelieved and denied Our verses and the meeting of the Hereafter, those will be brought into the punishment [to remain].

items.verse: فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَعَقَرُوهَا فَدَمْدَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ رَبُّهُم بِذَنۢبِهِمْ فَسَوَّىٰهَا
items.verseEn: But they denied him and hamstrung her. So their Lord brought down upon them destruction for their sin and made it equal [upon all of them].
So they will be overturned into it [i.e., Hellfire], they and the deviators
And when the [contents of] graves are scattered [i.e., exposed],

items.verse: أَمْ عِندَهُمْ خَزَآئِنُ رَبِّكَ أَمْ هُمُ ٱلْمُصَۣيْطِرُونَ
items.verseEn: Or have they the depositories [containing the provision] of your Lord? Or are they the controllers [of them]?

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ هِيَ جُزْءٌ مِنْ خَبَرِ 'إِنَّ'
categories.nameEn: Words that are part of the predicate (khabar) of the particle 'inna'

items.verse: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ نَزَّلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۗ وَإِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱخْتَلَفُوا۟ فِى ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لَفِى شِقَاقٍۭ بَعِيدٍۢ
items.verseEn: That is [deserved by them] because Allāh has sent down the Book in truth. And indeed, those who differ over the Book are in extreme dissension.

items.verse: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَخَّرَ لَكُم مَّا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱلْفُلْكَ تَجْرِى فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ بِأَمْرِهِۦ وَيُمْسِكُ ٱلسَّمَآءَ أَن تَقَعَ عَلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِۦٓ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِٱلنَّاسِ لَرَءُوفٌۭ رَّحِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: Do you not see that Allāh has subjected to you whatever is on the earth and the ships which run through the sea by His command? And He restrains the sky from falling upon the earth, unless by His permission. Indeed Allāh, to the people, is Kind and Merciful.

items.verse: وَءَاتَىٰكُم مِّن كُلِّ مَا سَأَلْتُمُوهُ ۚ وَإِن تَعُدُّوا۟ نِعْمَتَ ٱللَّهِ لَا تُحْصُوهَآ ۗ إِنَّ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ لَظَلُومٌۭ كَفَّارٌۭ
items.verseEn: And He gave you from all you asked of Him. And if you should count the favor [i.e., blessings] of Allāh, you could not enumerate them. Indeed, mankind is [generally] most unjust and ungrateful.

harf.hint: An ancient kingdom ruled by a powerful queen.
harf.arabicVerse: لَقَدْ كَانَ لِسَبَإٍۢ فِى مَسْكَنِهِمْ ءَايَةٌۭ ۖ جَنَّتَانِ عَن يَمِينٍۢ وَشِمَالٍۢ ۖ كُلُوا۟ مِن رِّزْقِ رَبِّكُمْ وَٱشْكُرُوا۟ لَهُۥ ۚ بَلْدَةٌۭ طَيِّبَةٌۭ وَرَبٌّ غَفُورٌۭ
harf.verse: 34:15 — There was for [the tribe of] Saba’ in their dwelling place a sign: two [fields of] gardens on the right and on the left. [They were told], "Eat from the provisions of your Lord and be grateful to Him. A good land [have you], and a forgiving Lord."

deduction.title: The March of Futility
deduction.intro: A mighty army, confident in its power and led by a colossal beast, set out with a singular, destructive purpose. They believed their sheer force could desecrate a sacred sanctuary, but they were about to encounter a power far greater than their own. Can you uncover the identity of this arrogant legion and the divine intervention that thwarted their plan?
clues: We were a great multitude, traveling with a creature of immense size, certain of our victory.
Our leader, driven by envy, sought to divert the world's reverence from a sacred house to one he had built.
As we approached our target, our largest animal refused to advance, kneeling in submission to a will greater than our own.
We did not face a human army; instead, our doom came from the sky, carried by the smallest of messengers.
Swarms of birds appeared above us, each carrying stones that would seal our fate.
Our army was left like 'eaten straw,' a lesson for all who would challenge the sanctity of the Kaaba in the year of the Prophet's birth.

trial.label: What Was Our Mission?
options: To flood the city of Saba'
To build a tower to the heavens

location.label: What Was Sent Against Us?
options: A shower of fire and brimstone
A swarm of locusts and lice

outcome.label: What Was Our Fate?
options: Annihilated by stones of baked clay
Forced to retreat in a storm
outcome.answer: Annihilated by stones of baked clay

deduction.verse: And He made them like eaten straw. (105:5)

translations: And why (did you) not when you entered your garden
(there is) no power except with Allah
scramble.hint: A phrase to say upon admiring one's blessings, attributing all power to Allah.
scramble.arabic: وَلَوْلَآ إِذْ دَخَلْتَ جَنَّتَكَ قُلْتَ مَا شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ لَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِٱللَّهِ ۚ إِن تَرَنِ أَنَا۠ أَقَلَّ مِنكَ مَالًۭا وَوَلَدًۭا
scramble.verseEn: And why did you, when you entered your garden, not say, 'What Allāh willed [has occurred]; there is no power except in Allāh'? Although you see me less than you in wealth and children,
